title: Bigelow Institute Essay Competition 2025
description: Annual essay competition seeking the best evidence for the survival of human consciousness after permanent bodily death. Open to researchers worldwide with no institutional affiliation required. Previous competitions awarded $1M+ across 29 finalists.
eligibility: Open to all researchers worldwide. No institutional affiliation required.

title: SCU Annual Conference 2025 - Call for Papers
description: The Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies invites paper submissions for its annual technical conference. Presentations should employ rigorous scientific methodology applied to UAP cases or related physical analysis. Peer-review process applied to all submissions.
eligibility: Open to scientists, engineers, and researchers. Papers must meet SCU peer-review standards.

title: Galileo Project Research Fellowship
description: The Galileo Project at Harvard University periodically accepts applications from researchers interested in joining its data analysis team. Work involves analysis of sensor data from the Harvard Yard Observatory array, including infrared, optical, radio, and audio channels.
eligibility: Graduate students and postdoctoral researchers with backgrounds in astrophysics, data science, or instrumentation.

title: SOL Foundation Symposium 2025 - Speaker Submissions
description: The SOL Foundation invites speaker submissions for its 2025 annual symposium at Stanford University. Presentations should advance scientific understanding of UAP phenomena, with particular interest in materials analysis, consciousness research, and policy frameworks.
eligibility: Researchers and policy professionals with peer-reviewed publications or demonstrated research in UAP-adjacent fields.

title: UAPx Field Research Collaboration
description: UAPx seeks collaboration with sensor engineers, data scientists, and marine researchers for upcoming field deployments. Instrumented observation platforms operating in regions of historically high UAP activity near Puerto Rico and the Caribbean.
eligibility: Researchers with expertise in radar systems, optical sensors, or marine environment observation.

title: Bigelow Institute Essay Competition 2024
description: 2024 cycle of the Bigelow Institute annual essay competition. Topic: best evidence for survival of human consciousness after permanent bodily death.

title: Parapsychological Association Research Grants
description: The Parapsychological Association awards research grants to support empirical investigation of psi phenomena including ESP, psychokinesis, anomalous cognition, and related topics. Grants are intended to support early-career researchers and seed projects that can lead to peer-reviewed publication. Preference given to methodologically rigorous designs with pre-registration.
eligibility: Open to researchers at any career stage. No institutional affiliation required, though affiliation strengthens applications.

title: Journal of Scientific Exploration - Ongoing Submissions
description: The Journal of Scientific Exploration accepts ongoing manuscript submissions on anomalous phenomena including UAP, parapsychology, consciousness research, and anomalistics. One of the few peer-reviewed journals with an explicit mandate to consider empirically rigorous UAP research. Submissions are evaluated through double-blind peer review by domain experts.
eligibility: Open to researchers worldwide. Manuscripts must meet peer-review standards and include reproducible methodology.

title: FREE Experiencer Survey - Ongoing Participation
organization: Foundation for Research into Extraterrestrial and Extraordinary Experiences
description: FREE's ongoing survey research collecting data from individuals who have had UAP contact, anomalous, or extraordinary experiences. The survey covers a comprehensive range of experiencer phenomena and has accumulated over 4,000 respondents since 2012. Data contributes to the largest academic dataset on contact phenomenology. Anonymous participation.
eligibility: Open to any individual who has had a UAP contact or extraordinary experience. Anonymous; no personal identification required.

title: IRVA Remote Viewing Research Studies
description: IRVA-affiliated researchers periodically recruit participants for controlled remote viewing experiments. Studies test anomalous information transfer under double-blind conditions following the protocols developed at SRI International. Participants with and without prior remote viewing training are sought.
eligibility: Open to adults worldwide. No prior remote viewing training required; both naive and trained participants are valuable for controlled studies.

title: Limina: Journal of UAP Studies - Ongoing Submissions
description: Limina: The Journal of UAP Studies accepts ongoing manuscript submissions on UAP phenomena across all disciplines - physics, social science, philosophy, history, policy, and psychology. The only peer-reviewed academic journal exclusively dedicated to UAP research. Published through Taylor & Francis with open-access options.
eligibility: Open to researchers worldwide across all disciplines. Manuscripts must meet peer-review standards. Interdisciplinary submissions particularly encouraged.
